Patch Set 14:

>For the third time: This is very indirect. Why can't MDTs reject creates in unlinked striped directories?

Sorry, missed your question when update the patch.  I think this fix is before we add that "trick" to 9511, i.e. master mark the dead of slave stripe by some tricky way. 

Since that is a little tricky solution, I am not sure if we need that when I tried to fix this problem, and find out  deny the layout retrieval can fix the issue you raised in the ticket. But since 9511 is landed, probably this patch might not needed anyway, but the test case might still need. I will merge it with some later patch(dir layout lock patch).

Abandoned

This patch has been merged to the fix in LU-4930

parents.subject: LU-4603 lmv: a few fixes about readdir of striped dir.

commit.subject: LU-4869 mdt: Do not retrieve dir layout for unlinked dir
commit.message:
LU-4869 mdt: Do not retrieve dir layout for unlinked dir

1. Do not retrieve MD layout if directory has been
unlinked, then the client will be notified the directory
is unlinked when it revalidate the inode, so to avoid
other client, who still cache the layout, will create
files/directories under slave stripes.

2. Add a few tests to check the process.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I6bc73d879075c2ee72a2efb211e51a8525d073ee
